name: Diamond Exchange
description_short: Independent jewelry retailer in Columbus since 1923 offering appraisals, repairs, custom design, and estate buying services for diamonds and watches.
description_long:
Columbus Diamond Exchange is a historic, independently owned jewelry business established in 1923 and based at 22 West Gay Street in downtown Columbus, Ohio. With over 100 years of operation, the company has positioned itself as a full-service jewelry retailer with deep roots in the local community. The business operates during standard retail hours: weekdays 10 AM to 5:30 PM and Saturdays 10 AM to 4 PM, with a dedicated staff available to assist customers.

The company offers a comprehensive range of jewelry-related services beyond basic retail sales. These include jewelry appraisals, watch repair and battery replacement, stone setting, custom design work, jewelry repair, engraving, and estate buying services. They also provide a concierge service and free cleaning and inspection of jewelry. According to their website, customers are encouraged to call (614) 228-2002 for pricing and assistance with any of their in-house services.

Columbus Diamond Exchange distinguishes itself primarily through its longevity and established reputation in the local market. The company emphasizes its independent ownership status and positions itself as a full-service operation capable of meeting diverse customer needs. Their estate buying service indicates they engage in the secondary market for jewelry, which aligns with pawn-shop category functions.

However, the company's online presence is limited. The website provides basic information but lacks detailed service descriptions, pricing transparency, customer reviews, or testimonials. There is no clear information about loan products, collateral-based lending terms, or how their estate buying service works. The business appears to function primarily as a traditional jewelry retailer with repair services rather than as an active pawn or lending operation, though this cannot be definitively determined from the available website content.
pros: Over 100 years of continuous operation since 1923 provides established credibility and local market presence
Comprehensive in-house services including appraisals, custom design, watch repair, engraving, and jewelry repair
Estate buying service enables customers to sell jewelry directly to the business
Free cleaning and inspection service included for customers
Independent ownership structure rather than corporate chain
Dedicated staff available for consultation via phone (614) 228-2002
Historic downtown Columbus location at 22 West Gay Street
cons: No pricing information published online; customers must call for quotes on services or purchases
Website lacks customer reviews, testimonials, or detailed service descriptions
No transparency about loan terms, rates, or collateral-based lending products if offered
Limited online presence with minimal social media engagement visible
Unclear whether company actively functions as a pawn shop with lending services or primarily as a jewelry retailer
best_for: Customers seeking jewelry appraisals, custom design, or specialized repairs from an established local jeweler
Individuals looking to sell estate jewelry or diamonds to an independent local business
Columbus residents needing watch repair or jewelry maintenance services from a historically established vendor
diagnosis: Columbus Diamond Exchange is best for customers in the Columbus area seeking jewelry services from an established, independent retailer with over a century of local history. The main caveat is that the website does not clearly indicate whether the company operates as a traditional pawn shop with collateral-based lending, or functions primarily as a jewelry retailer with repair and buying services—customers must call directly to understand all available products and terms.
